refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency compatible with safety. It is solution-neutral, specifying 'compatible with safe remote operation' without prescribing how to achieve it. The refinement clarifies the latency requirement as ensuring safety, maintaining problem domain focus and atomicity.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the 95th percentile latency is less than or equal to 150 ms, ensuring compliance with safety standards.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency end-to-end data transfer
Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering
Incorporate adaptive data compression and prioritization to reduce transmission delays
Establish a dedicated communication channel with guaranteed quality of service (QoS) parameters
Use predictive control algorithms to compensate for latency in teleoperation feedback loops
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication protocol and data processing modules
Dependency on network conditions affecting multiple system components
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on system architecture to minimize latency, which supports independence from specific communication protocols or network implementations, reducing coupling risk.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency requirements
Operational policies and safety plans are strictly followed to mitigate risks from latency variations
Predictive control or compensation mechanisms are not explicitly included, which may be needed if latency occasionally exceeds thresholds
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive or predictive control mechanisms in the DP to handle latency variations and enhance safety under less ideal network conditions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system architecture responsible for real-time data processing and minimal buffering, integrating necessary components and managing data flows.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional data exchange for real-time telemetry data essential for processing.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Network communication interface supporting real-time data transmission with minimal latency.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as NGHE Solution is the central system handling real-time data processing; minimal diffusion as most processing is internal.
Vehicle module handles physical operations; real-time processing partially relevant but would cause diffusion due to hardware focus.
Teleoperation involves remote control which requires some real-time data but is narrower in scope; partial containment with moderate diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring; real-time processing relevant but limited, leading to poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP as it is designed to handle core system architecture and data processing tasks, ensuring high cohesion and minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation for some real-time data processing tasks may cause interface complexity.
Integration with Vehicle module may require careful interface management to avoid latency issues.
Safety Services may require real-time alerts that need coordination, risking diffusion if not well defined.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine the DP to explicitly separate real-time data processing core functions from teleoperation and safety alert functions to improve modular clarity and reduce interface complexity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by focusing on a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering, which directly supports achieving the required teleoperation latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) for safe remote operation.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation module for some real-time data processing tasks may cause interface complexity and diffusion risk.
Integration with Vehicle module may introduce diffusion if real-time processing responsibilities are not clearly separated.
Safety Services requiring real-time alerts could cause diffusion if their processing overlaps with the NGHE Solution module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and minimal diffusion (containment score 8, diffusion score 2), making it an appropriate IM boundary for the DP. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the primary implementation module.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring end-to-end teleoperation latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) using the NGHE Solution module's telemetry and network link interfaces under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution IM is clear, as it encompasses the core system architecture responsible for real-time data processing and minimal buffering, directly impacting teleoperation latency.

recommended_revisions: Refine the DP to explicitly separate core real-time data processing functions from teleoperation control and safety alert functions to improve modular clarity and reduce interface complexity.
Define clear interface boundaries and responsibilities between NGHE Solution, Teleoperation, Vehicle, and Safety Services modules to mitigate diffusion risks.
Consider specifying sub-DPs or sub-modules within NGHE Solution to handle distinct real-time processing tasks to enhance cohesion and reduce diffusion.

decision.justification: All gates have passed, indicating that the current FR, DP, and IM mappings are logically sound and feasible for verification. However, the recommended revisions highlight risks of diffusion and interface complexity among modules, especially concerning real-time data processing tasks. To address these risks and improve modular clarity, it is prudent to decompose the current FR into next-level sub-FRs that explicitly separate core real-time data processing, teleoperation control, and safety alert functions. This will facilitate defining clearer interface boundaries and responsibilities, reducing diffusion and enhancing cohesion within the NGHE Solution module and across related modules.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Real-time data processing with minimal buffering to support teleoperation latency requirements
FR2: Teleoperation control ensuring safe remote operation within latency constraints
FR3: Safety alert generation and delivery with real-time responsiveness

revise.why: Refine the DP to explicitly separate core real-time data processing functions from teleoperation control and safety alert functions to improve modular clarity and reduce interface complexity, as recommended by gate results.

assumptions: The current FR adequately captures the high-level teleoperation latency requirement.
The NGHE Solution module is the primary implementation module responsible for real-time data processing.
Verification of latency through the NGHE Solution module is feasible under agreed network conditions.
open_risks: Potential diffusion and interface complexity among NGHE Solution, Teleoperation, Vehicle, and Safety Services modules.
Overlap of real-time processing responsibilities causing integration challenges.
Need for clear interface definitions to mitigate diffusion risks.
evidence_needed: Detailed functional decomposition of real-time processing, teleoperation control, and safety alert functions.
Interface definitions and responsibilities among involved modules.
Verification plans for sub-FRs to ensure latency and safety requirements are met.
